steps.title: Identify what can be 'very positive'
steps.content: The key signal is “has been very positive.” This tells us we need a noun describing something about the book that can be evaluated as positive or negative. The supporting evidence—reviewers praising it and it winning an award—confirms we're looking for something public about the book.

steps.title: Match the meaning to the choices
steps.content: ”Reception” names how a work is received and evaluated by critics and the public. The other three are illogical—they either reverse the focus (inspiration), contradict the positive evaluation (neglect), or don't fit the context (confusion). That confirms B is correct.
